decks.title: Friend Court
decks.description: Silly friend disputes for dramatic living-room justice.

cases.prompt: Ate the last slice of pizza and left the box as a decoy.
cases.plaintiffHint: Describe the betrayal of opening an empty box.
cases.defendantHint: Argue the slice was clearly abandoned property.

cases.prompt: Sent twelve voice notes instead of one text.
cases.plaintiffHint: Demand compensation for the minutes you'll never get back.
cases.defendantHint: Insist tone cannot be conveyed in text.

cases.prompt: Claimed aux cord power, then played one song on repeat.
cases.plaintiffHint: Read the play count into evidence.
cases.defendantHint: Call it a curated experience.

cases.prompt: Borrowed a hoodie and started calling it vintage.
cases.plaintiffHint: Produce the original receipt with feeling.
cases.defendantHint: Argue you've improved its story.

cases.prompt: Spoiled a show finale with suspiciously specific hints.
cases.plaintiffHint: Recreate the moment your jaw dropped — in horror.
cases.defendantHint: Claim it was technically a guess.

cases.prompt: Arrived late but blamed the group chat vibes.
cases.plaintiffHint: Enter the timestamps as exhibit A.
cases.defendantHint: Blame the energy, not the clock.

cases.prompt: Took the best selfie angle and cropped everyone else badly.
cases.plaintiffHint: Hold up the cropped evidence.
cases.defendantHint: Argue you were protecting their privacy.

cases.prompt: Invented house rules halfway through a board game.
cases.plaintiffHint: Cite the original rulebook with conviction.
cases.defendantHint: Claim the rules were always implied.

cases.prompt: Finished the shared fries while maintaining eye contact.
cases.plaintiffHint: Re-enact the eye contact for the jury.
cases.defendantHint: Argue hesitation forfeits the fry.

cases.prompt: Said 'five minutes away' while still choosing shoes.
cases.plaintiffHint: Present the 40-minute wait as a hardship.
cases.defendantHint: Argue footwear is a serious decision.

decks.title: Family Tribunal
decks.description: Low-stakes household squabbles with high-stakes speeches.

cases.prompt: Changed the thermostat and denied touching it.
cases.plaintiffHint: Describe waking up in a tundra.
cases.defendantHint: Suggest the house has a mind of its own.

cases.prompt: Used the last clean towel without starting laundry.
cases.plaintiffHint: Mourn the cold, dripping aftermath.
cases.defendantHint: Argue laundry is everyone's job.

cases.prompt: Ate leftovers clearly labeled with another person's name.
cases.plaintiffHint: Hold up the sticky note for the jury.
cases.defendantHint: Claim the label had faded beyond reading.

cases.prompt: Paused a movie every two minutes to explain the plot.
cases.plaintiffHint: Tally the interruptions dramatically.
cases.defendantHint: Argue you were providing premium commentary.

cases.prompt: Loaded the dishwasher like a modern art installation.
cases.plaintiffHint: Describe the chaos with great sadness.
cases.defendantHint: Claim it's a bold new technique.

cases.prompt: Told an embarrassing childhood story to new guests.
cases.plaintiffHint: Object to the betrayal of trust.
cases.defendantHint: Argue the story was simply too good to hold.

cases.prompt: Moved the remote to a secret second location.
cases.plaintiffHint: Recreate the frantic 20-minute search.
cases.defendantHint: Claim the remote belongs where you left it.

cases.prompt: Declared a snack 'for everyone' after eating most of it.
cases.plaintiffHint: Present the nearly-empty bag.
cases.defendantHint: Argue 'everyone' included several of you.

cases.prompt: Started a group photo after everyone had changed clothes.
cases.plaintiffHint: Describe re-doing your whole outfit.
cases.defendantHint: Claim the lighting demanded it.

cases.prompt: Reheated fish and called it an act of courage.
cases.plaintiffHint: Describe the lingering aroma in detail.
cases.defendantHint: Argue good food is worth the consequences.

decks.title: Office Chaos Court
decks.description: Workplace comedy cases — the work-safe deck for team icebreakers.

cases.prompt: Replied all with 'thanks' and created a notification avalanche.
cases.plaintiffHint: Count the inboxes disturbed.
cases.defendantHint: Argue gratitude is never wrong.

cases.prompt: Booked a meeting that could have been a three-word message.
cases.plaintiffHint: Mourn the lost 30 minutes.
cases.defendantHint: Claim alignment requires a room.

cases.prompt: Took the last donut and left the empty box in the break room.
cases.plaintiffHint: Present the tragic empty box.
cases.defendantHint: Argue someone else should have restocked.

cases.prompt: Said 'quick sync' and scheduled forty-five minutes.
cases.plaintiffHint: Define 'quick' for the record.
cases.defendantHint: Argue depth takes time.

cases.prompt: Renamed a shared file to 'final_final_REAL_final'.
cases.plaintiffHint: Describe the version-control nightmare.
cases.defendantHint: Argue it was, in fact, the real final.

cases.prompt: Mic stayed unmuted during a very crunchy snack break.
cases.plaintiffHint: Recreate the sound for the jury.
cases.defendantHint: Claim the mute button is unreliable.

cases.prompt: Borrowed a charger and built a permanent desk shrine around it.
cases.plaintiffHint: Demand the charger's return.
cases.defendantHint: Argue possession is nine-tenths of the desk.

cases.prompt: Used corporate jargon to describe ordering lunch.
cases.plaintiffHint: Translate 'circle back on the sandwich.'
cases.defendantHint: Argue synergy applies to deli meats too.

cases.prompt: Set a calendar invite for 4:59 PM on Friday.
cases.plaintiffHint: Describe your weekend dissolving.
cases.defendantHint: Argue the week wasn't over yet.

cases.prompt: Left one sip of coffee in the pot to avoid making more.
cases.plaintiffHint: Hold up the nearly-empty pot.
cases.defendantHint: Argue one sip is technically not empty.

decks.title: Date Night Court
decks.description: Playful couple's disputes for two — or a giggling jury of friends.

cases.prompt: Said 'I don't care where we eat' and rejected nine restaurants.
cases.plaintiffHint: Read the rejected list aloud, one by one.
cases.defendantHint: Argue you cared about all the wrong ones.

cases.prompt: Promised 'one more episode' at midnight three nights running.
cases.plaintiffHint: Enter the sleep-deprivation evidence.
cases.defendantHint: Argue cliffhangers are legally binding.

cases.prompt: Took a photo for the gram and made it the whole date.
cases.plaintiffHint: Count the retakes for the jury.
cases.defendantHint: Argue the memory needed to be perfect.

cases.prompt: Stole the blanket and claimed to be 'a still sleeper'.
cases.plaintiffHint: Describe the 3 AM cold front.
cases.defendantHint: Argue the blanket migrated on its own.

cases.prompt: Ordered 'just a few fries' then ate half of yours.
cases.plaintiffHint: Present the fry deficit.
cases.defendantHint: Argue shared food has no owner.

cases.prompt: Planned a 'surprise' that was suspiciously their own hobby.
cases.plaintiffHint: Note whose interest it really served.
cases.defendantHint: Argue you were sharing your world.

cases.prompt: Said the playlist was 'for us' and it was all their songs.
cases.plaintiffHint: Scroll the track list as evidence.
cases.defendantHint: Argue you have superior taste, for both of you.

cases.prompt: Replied 'haha' to a heartfelt paragraph.
cases.plaintiffHint: Read the paragraph, then the reply.
cases.defendantHint: Argue 'haha' contains multitudes.

cases.prompt: Insisted they 'know a shortcut' and added forty minutes.
cases.plaintiffHint: Map the scenic detour with a sigh.
cases.defendantHint: Argue the views were worth it.

cases.prompt: Re-gifted a present back to the person who gave it.
cases.plaintiffHint: Reveal the familiar wrapping paper.
cases.defendantHint: Argue it's the thought that recirculates.
